content.text:
[cron:15028631-f68e-464d-980d-b71e15ab1c1a version save] run a sync to GitHub to save version history of this OpenClaw and all other programs and files
Current time: Saturday, May 9th, 2026 - 9:51 AM (America/Chicago) / 2026-05-09 14:51 UTC

Use the message tool if you need to notify the user directly with an explicit target. If you do not send directly, your final plain-text reply will be delivered automatically.
I'll kick off a sync of the workspace and relevant directories to GitHub. Let me first check what repos and sync scripts are already set up.

arguments.query: GitHub sync version history backup repository

arguments.command: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace && git remote -v 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; ls -la sync*.sh backup*.sh 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; cat HEARTBEAT.md 2>/dev/null | head -30

content.text: Good — workspace already has a GitHub remote (`github` → `landonking-gif/openclaw-workspace`). Let me check git status and also look for other directories that should be synced.

arguments.command:
# Check for other git repos that should be synced
find ~/.openclaw -maxdepth 2 -name ".git" -type d 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
# Check .agents directory
ls -la ~/.agents/ 2>/dev/null | head -20
echo "---"
# Check if .agents has a git repo
cd ~/.agents 2>/dev/null && git remote -v 2>/dev/null

content.text: Multiple repos to sync. Let me check each one for changes and the main openclaw config repo too.

arguments.command:
# Check all repos for pending changes
echo "=== workspace (main) ==="
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace && git status --short && git remote -v | grep push

echo ""
echo "=== openclaw-config ==="
cd ~/.openclaw && git status --short && git remote -v | grep push

echo ""
echo "=== agents ==="
cd ~/.agents && git status --short

echo ""
echo "=== alpha-manager ==="
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace-alpha-manager && git status --short && git remote -v 2>/dev/null | grep push

echo ""
echo "=== beta-manager ==="
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace-beta-manager && git status --short && git remote -v 2>/dev/null | grep push

echo ""
echo "=== gamma-manager ==="
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace-gamma-manager && git status --short && git remote -v 2>/dev/null | grep push

echo ""
echo "=== eta-manager ==="
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace-eta-manager && git status --short && git remote -v 2>/dev/null | grep push

echo ""
echo "=== workers ==="
for w in general-worker coding-worker infra-worker product-worker marketing-worker legal-worker; do
  cd ~/.openclaw/workspace-$w 2>/dev/null && echo "$w: $(git status --short | head -3)" || echo "$w: (not found)"
done

content.text: Three repos have changes: workspace, openclaw config, and agents. Let me commit and push all of them.

arguments.command:
# 1. Sync main workspace
cd ~/.openclaw/workspace
git add -A
git commit -m "🔄 auto-sync: version save $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%MZ)" --allow-empty-message 2>/dev/null || true
git push github main 2>&1

# 2. Sync openclaw config (sessions, cron, memory, settings)
cd ~/.openclaw
git add -A
git commit -m "🔄 auto-sync: version save $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%MZ)" 2>/dev/null || true
git push origin main 2>&1

# 3. Sync agents skills
cd ~/.agents
git add -A
git commit -m "🔄 auto-sync: version save $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%MZ)" 2>/dev/null || true
git push origin main 2>&1

content.text: Now let me also push the workspace to its local backup remote for redundancy:

arguments.command: cd ~/.openclaw/workspace && git push backup main 2>&1 && git push origin main 2>&1

content.text:
All repos synced. Here's the summary:

**✅ GitHub sync complete** — 2026-05-09 14:57 UTC

| Repo | Remote | Changes | Status |
|------|--------|---------|--------|
| **openclaw-workspace** | `github` → landonking-gif/openclaw-workspace | 1 file | ✅ pushed |
| **openclaw-workspace** | `backup` → local backup | 1 file | ✅ pushed |
| **openclaw config** | `origin` → landonking-gif/openclaw | 18 files (sessions, cron, memory, settings) | ✅ pushed |
| **openclaw-agents** | `origin` → landonking-gif/openclaw-agents | 1 file (doc-sync skill) | ✅ pushed |

Worker/manager workspaces were clean — no changes to push.
